patches.find: a three-layer disagreement on the headline "prospective_confirmed" count itself (10 / 9 / 1).
patches.replace: most sharply, a three-layer disagreement on the prospective_confirmed sub-count (10 / 9 / 1) — each publication of the same aggregate gives a different number.
patches.rationale: ISS-1163: TLDR said 'most sharply' then referred to the 10/9/1 count as the headline argument, but Part 4.6 is about API vs visible layer divergence more broadly; the 10/9/1 count is one of six gaps. Revised to accurately scope the count as a sub-count within the broader registry divergence argument.

patches.find: This audit is designed to shrink as the author propagates his API-level rigor to the visible layer. It will not shrink if he only edits the machine-readable files — which is the pattern we are documenting.
patches.replace: This audit is designed to shrink as the author propagates his API-level rigor to the visible layer. Two distinct closing mechanisms apply: some rows close automatically when API-level rendering catches up to a propagation lag (render-lag rows); others require an active edit to the HTML headline by the author (author-action rows). It will not shrink if he only edits the machine-readable files — which is the pattern we are documenting.
patches.rationale: ISS-1162: 'self-healing' framing could be attacked as including render-lag rows that auto-close without author action, making the audit look like it captures noise. Added explicit distinction between render-lag closure and author-action closure.

patches.find: The Nullarbor Plain straight-road segment deviates only 0.4% from the globe geodesic; most of the 6.2% RMSE measures road curviness, not dome geometry.
patches.replace: The Nullarbor Plain's dead-straight central segment approaches the globe geodesic with a road-to-geodesic ratio near 1.00 on ~146 km (per WIN-069); most of the 6.2% RMSE measures road curviness, not dome geometry.
patches.rationale: ISS-1153: '0.4%' figure was unsourced and inconsistent with WIN-069 which says ratio 'approaches 1.00 on ~146 km'. Replaced with WIN-069-sourced language.

patches.find: λg = 8,619 km is the primary shared constant linking the firmament-height, geomagnetic-decay, and equatorial-gravity rows.
patches.replace: λg = 8,619 km is the primary shared constant linking rows across firmament-height, geomagnetic-decay, and equatorial-gravity domain types (these three account for the majority of the 14 λg-dependent rows identified in the knob count above; the audit table below annotates each).
patches.rationale: ISS-1141: Saying lambda_g links 'three rows' while the text also says 14 of 28 are lambda_g-dependent was confusing. Clarified that these are three domain types among the 14 lambda_g-dependent rows, with pointer to the table.

patches.find: domain-inflation factor of roughly 2.33×. Twenty-eight labels cannot carry more independent information than the twelve knobs they are derived from; the excess is label multiplication. (An earlier version of this section reported "~10 knobs" and "2.8×"; the recount to 12 / 2.33× is the honest arithmetic against formula_runtime.json plus the three master constants.)
patches.replace: domain-inflation factor of roughly 2.33×. Twenty-eight labels cannot carry more independent information than the twelve knobs they are derived from; the excess is label multiplication. (An earlier version of this section reported "~10 knobs" and "2.8×"; the recount to 12 / 2.33× is the honest arithmetic against formula_runtime.json plus the three master constants. Note: 12 is the full formula-pack budget; the effective knobs engaged by the scored domains are approximately 9–10, making 2.33× a lower bound on domain inflation.)
patches.rationale: ISS-1143: Using full formula-pack budget as denominator understates inflation. Added note that 12 is the full budget and ~9-10 effective makes 2.33x a lower bound.
